---
title: "data modeling by Yaxing Li"
author: "Yaxing Li"
date: "2025-03-07"
output: html_document
---

# Modeling

## 1.Preparation of data and packages
```{R}
library("tidyverse")
library("tidymodels")
library(shapviz)  # Needs development version 0.9.0 from github
sessionInfo()
data <- read_csv("final_engineered_data.csv")
```

## 2.transforming the label variable
```{R}
data |>  dim()
data |> head()
# Split the data into training set
train_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "train") %>% 
  mutate(label=factor(label, levels=c('baseline','meditation','stress','break')))  # Convert the label column to factor

test_data <- data %>% 
  filter(split == "test")

```

## 3. check participants intersect
```{R}
train_subjects <- train_data%>% select(participant_id) %>% distinct()
test_subjects <- test_data %>% select(participant_id) %>% distinct()
intersect(train_subjects, test_subjects)
```

## 4.Train-Test split
```{r}
set.seed(0) 
# Remove 'split' from the dataset before creating the recipe
train_data_clean <- train_data %>% select(-split)
# Split the data
data_split <- group_initial_split(train_data_clean, prop = 5/6, group = participant_id) 
train_split_data <- training(data_split)
val_split_data <- testing(data_split)
```

## 5. Create the workflow
```{R}
# Load the libraries
library(xgboost)
library(tidymodels)
library(dplyr)
library(yardstick)

# Define a preprocessing recipe
train_recipe <- recipe(label ~ ., data = train_split_data) %>%
  update_role(chunk_id, new_role = "ID") %>%  
  update_role(participant_id, new_role = "ID") %>%
  step_naomit(all_predictors()) %>%             # Remove missing values
  step_mutate_at(all_numeric_predictors(), fn = ~replace(., is.infinite(.), NA)) %>%  # Replace Inf with NA
  step_impute_knn(all_numeric_predictors(), neighbors = 20) %>% # KNN imputation
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%  # Normalize numerical features
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%      # One-hot encode categorical variables
  step_corr(all_numeric_predictors(), threshold = 0.8) %>%  # Remove highly correlated features
  step_zv(all_predictors()) %>%                 # Remove zero-variance predictors
  # step_rm(mean_rate_of_change, bpm_predicted_from_scr_peaks) |>  # Remove specified features
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), num_comp = 5)  # Reduce dimensionality with PCA

# Prepare the recipe
prepped_recipe <- prep(train_recipe, training = train_split_data)

# Apply transformations to the training and validation data
train_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = train_split_data)
val_processed <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = val_split_data)

# Remove non-predictor columns (e.g., 'label', 'chunk_id', etc.)
X_train <- train_processed %>%
  select(-label, -chunk_id, -participant_id)  # Adjust based on your dataset

X_val <- val_processed %>%
  select(-label, -chunk_id, -participant_id)  # Adjust based on your dataset

# Convert to matrix format for XGBoost
X_train_matrix <- as.matrix(X_train)
X_val_matrix <- as.matrix(X_val)

# Convert the target variable to numeric (0/1 for classification)
y_train_vector <- as.numeric(train_processed$label)
y_val_vector <- as.numeric(val_processed$label)
```

## Define the XGBoost Model and Workflow
```{R}
# Define the XGBoost model with hyperparameters to tune
xgb_model <- boost_tree(
  mode = "classification",  # Classification task
  trees = tune(),           # Tune number of trees
  tree_depth = tune(),      # Tune tree depth
  learn_rate = tune(),      # Tune learning rate
  min_n = tune(),           # Tune minimum samples in a node
  loss_reduction = tune(),  # Regularization parameter
  sample_size = tune()      # Subsampling ratio
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost")

# Define the workflow
xgb_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(xgb_model) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe)

```

## Tune the hyperparameters
```{R}
library(tune) 
# Define the search space for Bayesian optimization
xgb_param_set <- parameters(
  trees(range = c(100, 500)),         # Number of trees
  tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),        # Max depth of trees
  learn_rate(range = c(0.01, 0.3)),    # Learning rate
  min_n(range = c(5, 20)),             # Minimum samples per node
  loss_reduction(range = c(0, 10)),    # Regularization parameter
  sample_prop(range = c(0.5, 1.0))     # FIXED: Use sample_prop() for proportions
)

# Bayesian optimization control settings
bayes_control <- control_bayes(
  no_improve = 5,   # Stop after 5 rounds without improvement
  verbose = TRUE    # Print optimization progress
)

# Perform Bayesian optimization with 10-fold cross-validation
xgb_tune_results <- tune_bayes(
  xgb_workflow,
  resamples = vfold_cv(train_split_data, v = 10),
  param_info = xgb_param_set,  # Define the search space
  initial = 10,  # Start with 10 random configurations
  iter = 20,     # Run for 20 iterations
  metrics = metric_set(accuracy, roc_auc),  # Evaluate using accuracy and ROC AUC
  control = bayes_control
)

# View the best hyperparameters based on accuracy
best_xgb_params <- select_best(xgb_tune_results, metric = "accuracy")
print(best_xgb_params)
```

## Train the final model
```{R}
# Re-train the XGBoost model with the best parameters
final_xgb_model <- boost_tree(
  mode = "classification",
  trees = best_xgb_params$trees,
  tree_depth = best_xgb_params$tree_depth,
  learn_rate = best_xgb_params$learn_rate,
  min_n = best_xgb_params$min_n
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost")

# Update the workflow with the final model
xgb_workflow_final <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(final_xgb_model) %>%
  add_recipe(train_recipe)

# Train the final model on the whole training data
trained_xgb_model <- fit(xgb_workflow_final, data = train_split_data)
```

## Make Predictions and Evalute the Model
```{R}
# Make predictions on the validation data
val_predictions <- predict(trained_xgb_model, val_split_data)

# Bind predictions with the actual labels
val_predictions_final <- bind_cols(val_split_data, val_predictions)

# Evaluate the performance of the final model using multiple metrics
val_metrics <- val_predictions_final %>%
  metrics(truth = label, estimate = .pred_class)

# Print evaluation metrics
print(val_metrics)

```

## Model interpretation
```{R}
library(shapviz)
table(val_predictions$.pred_class)

## based on the classfication, I found it is difficult to distinguish between labels of meditation and break.

# Extract the fitted model's engine (XGBoost)
xgb_fit_engine <- extract_fit_engine(trained_xgb_model)

# Make sure the column names in the baked train data match the model's expected feature names
# The model uses the feature names from the recipe transformations (e.g., PCA, dummy variables)
baked_train_data <- bake(prepped_recipe, new_data = train_split_data)

# Ensure columns in the baked data match the ones used in the model
colnames(baked_train_data)

# The baked data should exclude columns like 'label', 'participant_id', 'chunk_id', etc.
X_pred <- baked_train_data %>%
  select(-label, -chunk_id, -participant_id)  # Adjust based on your dataset

# Convert to matrix format for the SHAP values calculation
X_pred_matrix <- as.matrix(X_pred)

# Extract SHAP values using shapviz
sv <- shapviz(xgb_fit_engine, X_pred = X_pred_matrix, X = X_pred)

# SHAP importance plot
svplot <- sv_importance(sv, kind = "beeswarm")
svplot

# Extract each individual plot from the list of plots
plot_baseline <- svplot[[1]]
plot_meditation <- svplot[[2]]
plot_stress <- svplot[[3]]
plot_break <- svplot[[4]]

# Optionally, adjust labels for clarity
svplot[[1]]$labels$title <- 'Baseline'
svplot[[2]]$labels$title <- 'Meditation'
svplot[[3]]$labels$title <- 'Stress'
svplot[[4]]$labels$title <- 'Break'

plot_break
plot_meditation
plot_stress
plot_baseline

# By analyzing the plots, I found the features are not useful or very confusing for distinguishing break and meditation sessions: min_bvp, mean_rate_of_change, mad_mag, mean_bvp and range_temp. So I will delete them in the recipe.
```

## fit model with out 2 features and PCA to the test data
```{R}
# Step 9: Train the final model on the entire training set
final_model <- fit(trained_xgb_model,  data = train_data_clean)
# Step 10: Make predictions on the test data
submission_data <- 
  augment(final_model, test_data, type = "class") %>% 
  select(participant_id, chunk_id, .pred_class)

# Save the predictions
submission_data %>%
  rename(label = .pred_class) %>%
  write.csv('DSW25_XGBOOS_model_with_5_PCA.csv', row.names = FALSE)
```
